message.content:
@startuml
title PB-428 UC12 View fund details

actor "Next Customer" as customer

rectangle "Next Platform" as platform {
  rectangle "Core Next" as coreNext
  database "Next Database" as db
}

rectangle "Core Bradesco" as bradesco

note top of customer
  **Abstract:**
  This use case will return fund details for a single fund. This is an existing endpoint and we will add additional response fields to this endpoint. They include:
  1. FundCategory (new)
  2. RequireFundTC (new)
  3. RiskScore (existing field in table, but needs to be added in response)
  4. IncomeValue (new)
  5. IncomeDescription (new)
  6. isNextSimples (new)
end note

customer -> coreNext: 1 HTTP GET /investments/funds/completeDetails

note right of customer
  The App will make a call to Core Next to get all the details for a fund. It will pass required input:
  - Fund ID
end note

note left of coreNext
  **Note to Dev**
  Please check if the input parameter Fund ID is the Product ID or the External_Core_Product_ID. From what I can see, there are two product records with the same External_Core_Product_ID (Next Simples) and this may create issues.
end note

coreNext --> coreNext: ref
note right of coreNext
  Use existing methodology to get complete details for a fund.
end note

coreNext -> db: 2 GET additional fields from Product table

db --> coreNext: 3 Response

note right of coreNext
  Core Next will retrieve additional data from the PRODUCT table and return them to the App:
  1. FundCategory (new)
  2. RequireFundTC (new)
  3. RiskScore (existing field in table, but needs to be added in response)
  4. IncomeValue (new)
  5. IncomeDescription (new)
  6. isNextSimples (new)
end note

coreNext --> customer: 4 Response

note left of customer
  Core Next will return all existing fields in the response + the following new response fields:
  1. FundCategory (new)
  2. RequireFundTC (new)
  3. RiskScore (existing field in table, but needs to be added in response)
  4. IncomeValue (new)
  5. IncomeDescription (new)
  6. isNextSimples (new)

  All of these fields will come from the PRODUCT table.
end note

@enduml
